name: SMILE Roadmap Synthesiser
description: Receives a ReadinessReport from the Readiness Analyst and generates a gap-targeted SMILE implementation roadmap. Calls smile_overview, smile_phase_detail (x2), and get_methodology_step to produce a phase-sequenced action plan where every phase explicitly states which readiness gap it addresses and cites the LPI methodology source.

supportedInterfaces.comment: Send ReadinessReport JSON to stdin. Requires npm run build and ollama serve.

inputSchema.description: ReadinessReport — output schema of the Digital Twin Readiness Analyst

skills.name: Gap-Targeted SMILE Roadmap
skills.description: Reads a ReadinessReport, identifies the 2 highest-severity gaps, selects the most relevant SMILE phases to close those gaps, deep-dives each via smile_phase_detail, and returns a concrete roadmap where every phase names the gap it targets. Provides first-week action checklist.
examples: Generate a SMILE roadmap for a project with low data maturity and poor stakeholder alignment
What SMILE phases should I prioritise for a solo ML engineer with no data pipeline?

security.inputValidation: ReadinessReport schema validated as first operation before any LPI calls.
security.injectionPrevention: All string fields from ReadinessReport re-sanitized before LLM use.
security.zeroTrust: Agent B validates independently — bypassing the orchestrator does not bypass security.

_lpiMetadata.explainability: Each roadmap phase includes evidence_source naming the smile_phase_detail call that grounded the activities. tools_used array records exact calls with char counts. request_id from upstream report is preserved for full trace.
_lpiMetadata.toolSplitRationale: Agent B uses only smile methodology tools (theory/prescription). It has no access to case studies or knowledge search — it only knows SMILE phases. Combined with Agent A, together they produce: specific gaps + targeted phase prescriptions.
